title: RUSTY review round 4: sieve-form post-round3-fixes
description: RUSTY idiomatic Rust review after round 3 fixes (Hash derives, windows idiom, take_while modifier scan, infallible expect). Checking if P0=P1=P2=0 and bikeshedding threshold reached.
notes:
lib+parse_error+form — Idiom rating: ACCEPTABLE — Three files are well-structured with correct ownership, proper error types, good iterator use, and no unsound patterns. Two P2 allocation/ergonomics issues remain (HashSet<&str>, From<Utf8Error>), plus minor redundant type annotations in form.rs. Nothing at P0 or P1.
lib+parse_error+form confidence: 90% chance of missed issue. Least confident: (1) for_each_clause clause_start edge cases — reviewed and appears correct; (2) two-pass require-scan vs check_extension_use collapsibility. SEC7: YES — running out of real complaints, findings are P2 and P3 only
evaluator+message+lexer — Idiom rating: ACCEPTABLE — three P2 allocation issues remain (expand_vars wasted alloc, regex cache key alloc per match, to_lowercase vs make_ascii_lowercase); no P0/P1 issues; API design is sound
evaluator+message+lexer confidence: 85% chance of missed issue. Least confident: str_matches_glob also builds base_key on every call (same pattern as str_matches_regex, possibly already captured under issue 6); quotewildcard double-replace (borderline P3, not filed). SEC7: YES (running out of real P0/P1 complaints, only P2 allocation micro-opts remain)
